commit.message:
Add auto-fix verification loop with Sandpack error detection

Wire reliable error detection and auto-fix into both the parallel build
pipeline and the ChatTab conversational flow. Previously, ChatTab had no
verification at all, and the parallel build used fragile DOM text scanning
with fixed timers.

Key changes:
- New Sandpack error store surfaces native sandpack.error globally
- Status-based settle waiting replaces fixed 3s timer
- Deterministic pre-validator catches missing imports/deps without AI
- ChatTab now triggers verification silently after every AI write
- /api/verify gets richer context (VFS file list, exports map, deps)
- Subtle verification indicator on Frame (Checking/Fixing/Verified)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade to Rust Edition 2024 and update all dependencies to latest

- Edition 2021 → 2024: rename `gen` (reserved keyword), add `unsafe extern`
- Modernize code with let-else patterns across 6 files
- eframe 0.31 → 0.33: `screen_rect()` → `content_rect()`, `fonts()` → `fonts_mut()`
- rodio 0.20 → 0.22: Sink → Player, OutputStream → MixerDeviceSink, Source yields f32
- windows 0.58 → 0.62: Option-wrapped params for GlobalFree/OpenClipboard/SetClipboardData
- zip 2 → 8, resvg 0.44 → 0.47, windows-core 0.58 → 0.62
- Add latest crate version policy to CLAUDE.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: native always-on Jarvis daemon with anti-interruption fixes

macOS background listener — no browser needed. Pipeline:
mic (sox) → Groq Whisper (free STT) → wake word → Groq Llama (free brain) → Edge TTS → afplay

- 15s active recording window (was 8s) so user can finish thoughts
- 3s silence threshold in active mode (was 1.5s) for natural speech pauses
- 3 silent rounds required before returning to passive mode
- System prompt instructs Jarvis to never interrupt mid-sentence
- Graceful shutdown with tmp file cleanup

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
Add missing data simulation for FASTA and MAPLE output

Simulate per-tip missing data from two sources: Poisson-distributed gaps
with exponentially-distributed lengths, and Poisson-distributed individual
missing sites. Gaps and sites are merged via a level-sweep algorithm into
non-overlapping ranges.

When missing data is active, complete (unmasked) sequences are written to
-COMPLETE files, while the normal output files reflect the missing data:
FASTA uses N for missing sites, MAPLE uses N ranges, and Nexus tip
annotations include missing_data_ranges. The info JSON gains a
missing_data section with parameters and per-tip details.

Missing data draws from the RNG after mutation simulation, so the same
seed produces identical complete sequences regardless of missing data
settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add: click map to set route endpoints

Three ways to set From/To without touching the dropdowns:

1. Click any waterway (canal/river) on the map
   → popup snaps to nearest town on that route
   → 'From here' (green) / 'To here' (red) buttons

2. Click any town marker → sidebar opens
   → 'Set as From' / 'Set as To' buttons at the top

3. Change the dropdowns in the planner panel
   → pins update automatically on the map

Visual feedback:
- Green teardrop pin (A) = From, red pin (B) = To
- Nudge bar floats at the bottom showing both endpoints
  and an 'Calculate Route' button — no need to open the planner
- Swap button (⇅) swaps both dropdowns AND the map pins

Pins persist when the planner panel is closed so endpoints
remain visible while exploring the map.

fix(api): Make disk check non-fatal and increase RSS threshold to 2GB

Disk usage on managed platforms (Railway/K8s) is outside app control
and should not cause 503 on /health/detailed. RSS threshold increased
from 1GB to 2GB since Node.js RSS includes native memory beyond heap.

fix: install slash commands to ~/.claude/commands/ instead of ~/.claude/skills/

Claude Code only recognizes slash commands in ~/.claude/commands/ (user-level)
or .claude/commands/ (project-level). The old ~/.claude/skills/ path was
invisible to Claude Code, so /tmux-* commands never appeared.

Also updates web-install.sh to use the same correct path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix receipt modal appearing and WhatsApp issues

**ROOT CAUSE:**
When receipt modal closed, it called onClose() which closed
the PARENT RecordSaleModalV2, unmounting everything and losing state.
This caused:
1. Receipt modal not appearing (state lost on unmount)
2. WhatsApp opening but losing receipt text (state destroyed)

**SOLUTION:**
- Removed onClose() call from receipt modal's onClose handler
- Now receipt modal closes independently
- Parent modal stays open, preserving all state
- User can reopen receipt, share multiple times, or close manually

**BENEFITS:**
✅ Receipt modal appears immediately after completing sale
✅ WhatsApp can open without destroying modal state
✅ User can share receipt multiple times (WhatsApp, email, copy, etc.)
✅ Better UX - explicit control over when to close

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): reduce code duplication to pass SonarCloud quality gate (#5492)

Extract shared audience table columns and phone mode content into
dedicated modules, fixing 13.1% duplication (required <= 3%).
Also fix useHandleValidation stuck checking state on fetch timeout.

Co-authored-by: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(engine): release Stuck friction limiter and early silence onset (v2.18)

p_stuck was structurally capped at ~5% due to low friction clamp
bounds (0.45/0.35) and late silence path onset (5s). Release clamp
to 1.0 with faster ramp divisors, lower silence threshold to 2s,
and smooth Incubation x=1 emission cliff (y=1: 0.10→0.08).
Stuck now reachable at ~30s sustained silence, enabling Nudge/Wall.

commit.message:
fix: make expiry selector functional and suppress init-time mailbox load error

- Re-enable TTL cleanup (only targets expires_at IS NOT NULL AND <= now)
- Backend handleGenerate/handleCreate now read expiry param and set expires_at
  - permanent → NULL (never expires), 1h/24h/3d → computed timestamp
- Existing mailboxes (expires_at = NULL) are never affected by cleanup
- Remove loadAllMailboxes() from admin init to prevent "加载邮箱失败" toast
  (still loads on-demand when switching to all-emails view)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix KPI bar — use PA/ERP-aware data (procurementSummary by_urgency)

Problem: KPI bar used criticalTanks (raw physical stock) so "20 Shortages"
showed while Optimus said "ALL CLEAR". Numbers were contradictory.

Changes:
- Added refs: kpiUncoveredShortages, kpiBelowThreshold, kpiTotalMonitored,
  kpiLowStationsPA (all populated from PA/ERP-aware data)

- procurementRes handler now reads by_urgency:
    Uncovered Shortages = CATASTROPHE+CRITICAL+MUST_ORDER+WARNING (action_needed)
    Below Threshold     = CATASTROPHE+CRITICAL only

- KPI template: criticalCount → kpiUncoveredShortages (label: "Uncovered Shortages")
                belowThresholdCount → kpiBelowThreshold (label: "Below Threshold")
                kpiLowStations → kpiLowStationsPA (from shortages unique station_ids)

- Header bar: now shows 3 states with matching color+icon:
    🔴 amber  — "{N} mandatory orders — decision required"
    🟡 yellow — "{N} positions to monitor"
    🟢 green  — "All stations operating normally"

- Low Stock Stations now counts unique stations with action_needed from
  shortages array (set in loadOptimus alongside analyzeNetwork call)

Result: Optimus ALL CLEAR → KPI shows 0/0/0/0, header green

commit.message:
fix(api): Return status 'up' with degraded flag for non-fatal health checks

Terminus v11 HealthCheckExecutor treats resolved values with
status 'down' as errors, causing 503 even when the promise doesn't
reject. Change storage/telegram catch handlers to return status 'up'
with a degraded flag so the overall /health/detailed endpoint stays
200 when only optional services are unavailable.

commit.message:
fix(spotify): derive username from artist name during import (#5500)

When importing a Spotify artist, the username was being set to the opaque
Spotify artist ID (e.g. "artist_5ymca6hmfvlhog3btjief0") instead of a
clean name derived from the artist (e.g. "fisher"). Now derives the
username from the Spotify artist name when available, with fallback to
the existing behavior if the name can't produce a valid handle.

commit.message:
Fix authentication and error handling issues from tester feedback

Issues addressed:
1. Save/Submit errors - Added comprehensive error handling with specific messages
2. Auth session expiration - Now detects expired sessions and prompts re-login with progress preservation
3. Cross-browser authentication - Added troubleshooting docs explaining cookie-based sessions
4. Password recovery - Added docs explaining Netlify Identity recovery process
5. Network errors - Added user-friendly network error messages and guidance

Changes:
- Enhanced storage.js with better token validation and specific error messages for 401/403/500/network errors
- Updated AssessmentForm.jsx handleSave() and handleSubmit() with:
  - Session expiration detection and recovery flow
  - SessionStorage backup before auth reload
  - Network error detection with user-friendly messages
  - Better console logging for debugging
- Added loadAssessment() error handling with user-friendly messages
- Added sessionStorage restore logic to recover progress after auth reload
- Added comprehensive Troubleshooting section to README.md covering:
  - Common error causes and solutions
  - Browser-specific issues (Safari cookie settings, Chrome incognito mode)
  - Cross-browser session behavior explanation
  - Password recovery troubleshooting
  - Technical support contact information

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

non-GET requests to prevent console errors during recipe imports and
other API mutations. Bump cache version to activate the update.
